criteria.name: cleaning.py runs on all raw data
criteria.evidence: cleaning.py exits 0 but 137 of ~142 files are SKIPPED with 'No aggregation rule defined for column' errors. Only 5 files from cavendish and greenwich succeed. The pipeline does not actually process all raw data — it silently skips most files.

criteria.name: Cleaned hourly + daily datasets in data/processed/
criteria.evidence: Pipeline test run produces output for only 2 of 5 stations (cavendish, greenwich). north_rustico, stanley_bridge, and tracadie have empty output directories. AC requires cleaned datasets for ALL stations discovered by the manifest.

criteria.name: Imputation report generated
criteria.evidence: imputation_report.csv is generated with audit records from impute_frame(). Code calls audit_trail_to_dataframe() and writes the CSV. Report exists in test output.

criteria.name: Stanhope reference data downloaded and cached
criteria.evidence: cleaning.py now calls materialize_stanhope_hourly_range() and normalize_stanhope_hourly(). The integration is present and structured correctly (wrapped in try/except, writes to data/processed/stanhope/).

criteria.name: FFMC, DMC, DC computed for all stations
criteria.evidence: _compute_fwi_codes() is called on the imputed hourly data, which only exists for 2 of 5 stations. For the 3 stations with no output, FWI codes are never computed. Additionally, FWI computation depends on columns air_temperature_c, relative_humidity_pct, wind_speed_kmh, rain_mm — must verify these exist in the resampled data.

criteria.name: ISI, BUI, FWI computed
criteria.evidence: fwi.py now contains initial_spread_index(), buildup_index(), and fire_weather_index() functions. cleaning.py imports and calls all three in _compute_fwi_codes(). The full FWI chain is implemented.

criteria.name: Pipeline artifacts manifest with timestamps and row counts
criteria.evidence: pipeline_manifest.json is written by _write_manifest() with generated_at timestamp, per-artifact timestamps, rows, station, type, and path fields. Manifest tracks 97 artifacts in test run.

summary: Significant progress since last review: cleaning.py now integrates imputation, Stanhope ingestion, full FWI chain (ISI/BUI/FWI added), and artifact manifest. However, the pipeline fails on most raw data files — 137 of ~142 files are skipped due to 'No aggregation rule defined for column' errors (Accumulated Rain, Barometric Pressure columns). Only 2 of 5 stations produce output. Additionally, the exit gate test (test_pipeline_execution.py) does not exist, and 2 existing tests fail (test_notebook_executes_without_error: FileNotFoundError on station_daily.csv; test_repo_root_shape_is_whitelisted: unexpected .cartography/ and codemap.md in repo root). The aggregation rule issue in src/pea_met_network/materialize_resampled.py must be fixed to handle Accumulated Rain and Barometric Pressure columns before the pipeline can satisfy AC-PIPE-1 and AC-PIPE-2.
